CHIEF JUSTICE
MALCOLM M, LUCAS
CHAIRPEFSO\

NCIL

CALIFORNIA
WILLIAM E DAV]S

ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS
303 2ND STREET, SOUTH TOWER
SAN FRANCISCO 94107 . (415) 396-9100
LEGISLATIVE OFFICE
8o1 K STREET. SUITE 18OO. SACRAMENTO 95814 . (9j6)44t752a
March 29, 19 91
Mr. John Mitchell
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Dear Mr. MitchelI:
I am requesting confinnation of the phone advice given to me by you
and by Jean Pritchard on March 26, l-991- and March 2A, 1991-
regarding my disclosure respons ibil ities 
with respect to ny
position as manager of the Family Court Servj-ces unit at the
Administrative Office of the Courts and my business and income to
the business.
The disclosure category that applies to rny position reads:
rrCategory L2. 
Investments and positions,t in and incorne from
business entitites 
that 
are providers** of 
family 
court
servi,ces ( including consulting and training) and related equiprnent
and suplies. tt *(Positions include ernployee and partner, officer,
director, 
trustee, 
and any other 
management position. )
**lProviders include business entities and individuals that are
manufacturers, distributors, vendors, se11ers, leasors, suppliers,
contractors, subcontractors, and other providers of the suppJ,ies,
equipment, real property, and services indicated in this category. )
The business j-s a sole propritorship and r.ras fomed in 1980 under
the title, 
"The Ne$, Farnily Centerrr. Since that tine it has been
treated as a separate business entity, with appropriate business
expenses, and deductions each year. The nature of the business is
that of providing consulting, educational , and counseling services
including written materiaLs with sorne appearances on a panels or as
a speaker for professional associations in the area of farnily taw,
family rnediation, individual and couple counseling, divorce, and
custody. Speechrnakj,ng is not the principle nature of the business.
The sources of the income to this business are outside of the state
of California and these sources do not do busi-ness $rith the AOc
unit. Income to this business does not exceed $l-O,OOO. from a
single source.


Uy understanding is that unless the income to this business from a
single source is greater than $1,0,000., it is not to be disclosed.
Further, it is rny understanding that the honoraria and gift limits
effective Jan. 1, 1,991- vrould not apply to this business as l-ong as
the work was done in connection with this bone fide business and
the predomj-nant activity 
was not that of naking speeches.
Therefore my statement on Forn 730 consists of Schedule A and
Schedule D. I have filed these statements with the AOC Personnel
unit L/hich j-s responsible for forwarding then to your office.
Thank you for your attention to this matter.
Ricci,

sections.question_synthetic: The sender is requesting written confirmation of prior phone advice regarding disclosure obligations for a position managing Family Court Services, specifically whether business income from a sole proprietorship providing consulting and counseling services must be disclosed under Category L2, and whether honoraria/gift limits apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a member of the public to FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document references prior phone advice but seeks written confirmation. Sender describes their understanding of disclosure requirements rather than asking a new question.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender is requesting written confirmation of prior phone advice regarding disclosure obligations for a position managing Family Court Services, specifically whether business income from a sole proprietorship providing consulting and counseling services must be disclosed under Category L2, and whether honoraria/gift limits apply.

embedding.summary: This is a letter from an AOC employee to FPPC requesting written confirmation of phone advice previously given regarding disclosure responsibilities for a business income from a sole proprietorship providing family law consulting and counseling services, with the understanding that income under $10,000 from a single source need not be disclosed.
